title: xyris longifolia
intro: Xyris longifolia, also known as the yellow-eyed grass, is a perennial plant species native to North America, characterized by its yellow flowers and grass-like leaves. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. Xyris longifolia is also valued for its health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds and coughs. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, and it can also be dried and used in infusions or decoctions.
